hypothesis: At 200–300 GPa, P63/mmc M4H52 (M = La, Sr, Ac) will exhibit two-gap superconductivity driven by weakly coupled phonon subspectra from the H24 clusters and H8-cube sheets, yielding Δlarge/Δsmall ≥ 1.3 at 0 K in anisotropic Eliashberg calculations, whereas coeval MH10 polymorphs will display single-gap behavior.
